output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 7/7.
Batch 1: Executed batch 1 task T1. Variant identity now survives the client-to-edge selected-clip path for timeline and gallery selections, supporting tests passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 2: Executed batch 2 task T2. Added server-side `ResolvedSelectionContext` resolution from the live timeline, updated `create_task` to use resolved selection entries for lineage lookup, verified gallery selections never resolve as timeline anchors, and wrote the batch checkpoint.
Batch 3: Executed batch 3 task T3. Source-derived editor-chat generation requests now persist `placement_intent` only for exactly one resolved timeline selection, `source_variant_id` now flows through the create-task input/resolver path, the affected edge suites passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 4: Executed batch 4 task T4. `complete_task` generation completion now returns normalized completion-asset metadata with `variant_id` for regenerated variants, source-derived variants, and standalone originals, the handler captures that enriched result for the next batch, the full `complete_task` module passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 5: Executed batch 5 task T5. `complete_task` now applies deterministic completion-time placement from `params.placement_intent` on the specified timeline, preserves the legacy `timeline_placement` path for older callers, records degraded follow-up issues instead of failing completion when placement cannot be applied, the full `supabase/functions/complete_task/` module passed, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 6: Executed batch 6 task T6. Added focused regression coverage for variant-aware selection normalization, source-derived task creation, and direct completion-time placement, all requested edge suites passed, the full `supabase/functions/complete_task/` module passed with the new `placement.test.ts`, and the batch checkpoint was written.
Batch 7: Executed batch 7 task T7. Fixed stale verification-only tests in the touched directories for the current `create-task` family contract and the newer `executeCreateTask(..., timelineId)` signature, reran the full affected test surface end to end, `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` passed, the throwaway integration script passed and was deleted, and the scoped diff was reviewed.
deviations: Added two supporting client wiring changes outside the listed file set: src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx to preserve variantId during merged selection serialization, and src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx to pass gallery variant metadata into the selection context.
Updated two adjacent client test files to verify the new serialized shapes: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx and src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx.
Ran the edge test with --config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts after confirming the default Vitest config excludes supabase/functions/**.

There is no `supabase/functions/tsconfig.json` in this workspace, so the TypeScript verification used the repo root config via `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json`.

Batch 7 required verification only, but the end-to-end rerun exposed stale tests in touched directories; this batch updated `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/create-task/index.test.ts`, and `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/__tests__/dispatch.test.ts` so the current API and mock surface match the implemented code.

The throwaway integration script could not be run with `tsx` in this sandbox because `tsx` attempted to open a blocked IPC pipe, so it was bundled with local `esbuild`, executed with `node`, and both temporary artifacts were deleted afterward.
Function suites were run with `--config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts` because the default app Vitest config excludes `supabase/functions/**`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added variant-aware selection identity across timeline selection resolution, gallery selection metadata, agent-session serialization, and edge normalization. Verified with the edge selectedClips suite plus adjacent client serialization/selection tests.

Added `ResolvedSelectionContext`, resolved it from the live timeline config on the edge, switched `create-task` to derive lineage from the resolved selection entries, and verified the resolver and create-task integration via the updated edge test files.

Added placement-intent persistence for source-derived editor-chat tasks, threaded `source_variant_id` into the resolver inputs, and verified the single-timeline-selection, gallery-only, and multi-selection branches with full edge test files.

Changed `createVariant()` to return the inserted variant id via `.select('id').single()`, threaded that id into normalized `CompletionAssetRef` values for regeneration, source-derived variants, and standalone originals, and verified the full `complete_task` module after updating the adjacent return-shape tests.

Added deterministic completion-time placement via `placement_intent`, covering anchor-found, fallback-track, and degraded no-anchor/no-track outcomes without failing task completion, and verified the full `complete_task` module.

Added focused regression coverage for variant-aware selection normalization, source-derived task creation, and completion-time placement, then re-ran the requested edge suites plus the full `complete_task` module with all tests passing.

Ran the full affected test surface end to end, updated stale verification-only tests for the current family-based create-task contract and `executeCreateTask(..., timelineId)` signature, verified the repo TypeScript check passes, executed and deleted a throwaway integration script for normalized selection and placement-intent shape, and reviewed the scoped diff.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified the round-trip end to end: useSelectedMediaClips now reads registry.variantId, AgentChat preserves variantId when timeline/gallery selections merge, useAgentSession emits selected_clips[].variant_id, ai-timeline-agent/types.ts accepts variant_id, and normalizeSelectedClips trims and preserves variant_id in the normalized payload.

Verified in code and tests that `resolveSelectionContext()` rejects `gallery-` clip ids as placement anchors and separately requires the clip id to exist in the live timeline config before setting `is_on_timeline=true`; gallery-prefixed and missing clip ids both resolve to `source='gallery'` with `is_on_timeline=false`.

Verified in create-task integration tests that `params.placement_intent` is emitted only when exactly one selected clip resolves with `is_on_timeline=true`; gallery-only selections and multi-selection timeline cases both omit `placement_intent` while still preserving `source_variant_id` where applicable.

Verified `createVariant()` now uses Supabase `.select('id').single()` and returns the inserted variant id, and traced that id into `CompletionAssetRef.variant_id` for `handleVariantCreation()`, regeneration, and standalone original-variant creation.

Verified in the handler-level completion tests that placement intent explicitly handles the live-anchor path, the anchor-missing fallback-track path, and the anchor-missing plus fallback-missing degraded path, and the handler records follow-up issues instead of failing task completion when placement cannot be applied.

Verified the updated suites assert on `variant_id` specifically across the revised contract: normalization preserves `variant_id`, create-task requests carry `source_variant_id` and `anchor_variant_id`, generation-handler tests already assert `CompletionAssetRef.variant_id`, and the new placement tests assert the registry entry stores `variantId` for the completed asset.

Verified the final end-to-end rerun passes across `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/`, `supabase/functions/create-task/`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, with the repo TypeScript check also passing after updating stale create-task and loop test expectations for the new return shapes and arguments.
